Appearances: Collin Kelley for and with Karen Huckobey and Debra Ferreira (Petitioners).

The First Account of Special Administrators is continued to April 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Examination Of Original Documents.
Off Calendar Jun-09-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This came on calendar for a forensic will examination pursuant to the court's May 5, 2016 order.
Appearances: Colin E. Kelley,Esq., appearing for Petitioners/Respondents Debra Ferreira and Karen Huckobey. Marlene Papas Getchell Esq., appearing for Contestant Carolyn Gaugert. Forensic Will Examiner Linton a. Mohammed, Ph D and Deputy Court Clerks Diane Hakewill, Gina R. S. Gonzalez and Lydia Paredes.
The will examination began promptly at 2:00 pm in the 202 conference room. Mr. Mohammed requested to see the one page holographic will. He scanned the will into his laptop, which he stated was to preview the will. He then used a microscope and flashlight to examine the front and back of the holographic will. He scanned the holographic will a second time in color at 300 ppi. The third time he scanned the will he scanned it at 1200 ppi because he stated he was scanning in the signatures.
Mr. Mohammed addressed Mr. Kelley to ask if he should focus on the signatures. Mr. Kelley stated the signatures were in dispute and requested that special attention should be given to the portions of the holographic will done in different color ink. Mr. Mohammed used an electronic microscope, which projected the image onto the laptop, to view the signatures. He then proceeded to use a non-electronic microscope and flashlight to examine the signatures and the name on the will. Mr. Mohammed then handed the holographic will back to the clerk.
Mr. Mohammed then requested to view the typographical will. He held the signature up against the light, and scanned in only the signature page. He then proceeded to use an electronic magnifying glass on the signatures and the image was projected on the laptop where he took snapshots of the signatures. He used the non electronic magnifying glass with a flashlight to examine the front and back of the page. Mr. Mohammed proceeded to hold page and two side by side. He held a flashlight behind page one and examined it. Throughout the will examination he took approximately one page of notes. The examination ended at 3:13 pm.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Will Examination
Off Calendar Dec-10-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This came on calendar for a forensic will examination pursuant to the court's 11-30-2015 order.

In Appearance: Marlene Pappas Getchell, Atty, appearing for petitioner Carolyn Gaugert, Meeghan Buckley, Atty, appearing for respondents Debra Ferreira, & Karen Huckobey, Forensic Will Examiner James A. Blanco, 415-300-7701, and Deputy Court Clerk Diane Hakewill

The will examination began at 1:14pm in the Room 202 conference room. The will examination proceeded in accordance with the 11/30/15 order.

Mr. Blanco color-scanned both the will and the codicil into his labtop computer. He proceeded to review the will with an electronic digital microscope, which was attached to his labtop. As the will was lying flat on the table, he used a LED flashlight and held it so the light came across the paper will pages sideways; he then viewed the will(s) with a flashlight and a magnifying glass.

With a digital camera mounted on a tripod mount with an extension rod directly over the flat-lying will, Mr. Blanco took over 30 images/pictures of the will(s).

The Deputy Court Clerk, Ms. Hakewill, was present during the entire will examination. She had the original and codicil in her view at all times. The will examination ended at 2:25pm.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-14-2015 Continued To Jan-20-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marlene Getchell for (petitioner) Carolyn Gaugert. Colin Kelly for (respondents) Debra Ferreira & Karen Huckobey.

The Court stated that we are here on competing petitions and positions on the validity of the 1991 holographic instrument and the effect on the 1974 will. Mr. Kelly stated that they have done discovery, that he is ready to file a Motion for Summary Judgment, that they are willing to discuss mediation and that the will speaks for itself. Ms. Getchell stated that they have not called in a handwriting expert yet, that they want the 1974 will admitted to probate and that the will had been concealed. The Court stated that it is not correct that the will had been concealed. Ms. Getchell stated that the parties have not talked and that they are going down the path of a runaway train. The Court stated that it is a legal question, that assuming the 1991 holographic will is a valid document it expressly excludes a piece of property and does that property get dealt with under the other will. Mr. Kelly stated that the carve out of the property does not get dealt with, that there is a note on the will regarding the property and that the scope of the will is different than the validity of the will. Ms. Getchell stated that the intent was that all decedent's property was included. Mr. Kelly stated that it is a two step process, 1 is this holographic and 2 what does it cover. The Court stated that it does not want to be faced with a Motion for Summary Judgment that can't procedurally go forward, asked if the parties were prepared to go forward with mediation, confirmed that the case did not qualify for pro bono mediation, that they will need a status conference date regarding the competing petitions and will contest and instructed the parties to talk to each other and to try and resolve the case. Ms. Getchell asked if the estate could pay for the mediation. The Court stated that it is not before the court. The two petitions for letters of administration with will annexed were continued to January 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the parties have tried to resolve the issues and have taken care of information exchanges and have a schedule of how to go forward.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
